applied_optimizations.name: fused_moe_triton config tuning for E=384 N=128 int4_w4a16
applied_optimizations.description: Created tuned Triton config files for Kimi K2.5 MoE kernel (E=384, N=128). Benchmarked hundreds of BLOCK_SIZE_M/N/K, GROUP_SIZE_M, num_warps, num_stages combinations per batch size. Best configs: M=32,N=128,K=64,G=4,w=4 for small batch; M=64,N=128,K=128,G=4,w=2 for medium; M=128,N=128,K=64,G=8,w=8 for large.

applied_optimizations.name: int4_w4a16 tuning infrastructure support
applied_optimizations.description: Added int4_w4a16 dtype support to tuning scripts, including uint8 packed tensor creation for int4 weights and N//2 adjustment for int4 packing.

applied_optimizations.name: Fix scaled_fp4_quant import error on ROCm
applied_optimizations.description: Wrapped scaled_fp4_quant import in try/except for ROCm where sgl_kernel doesn't have this function.

hotspot_targets.reason: Dominates GPU time at all batch sizes (92-97%). Default config causes pathological performance for E=384 with low tokens_per_expert.
hotspot_targets.next_step: Further tune M=512 and M=2048 configs where speedup is lowest (1.17x and 1.19x). Consider separate down-projection configs.

next_moves: Run full tuning sweep with existing tuning_fused_moe_triton.py for int4_w4a16
Tune separate down-projection configs (different memory access patterns)
Profile with rocprof --stats for detailed kernel timing breakdown
Try BLOCK_SIZE_N=256 for large batch sizes where shard_intermediate_size allows it
notes: torch.profiler showed fused_moe_kernel at 96.55% cuda_time for M=512, 92.74% for M=4096
rocprof profiling confirms kernel_time > 92% in fused_moe_kernel
ProfilerActivity.CUDA used for GPU kernel timing
Default config: BLOCK_SIZE_M=16, N=32, K=64, which is pathological for E=384
Best configs: M=32,N=128,K=64 for small batch, M=128,N=128,K=64 for large batch
Triton version 3.6.0, configs in triton_3_6_0 directory
GPU: AMD Instinct MI355X
Geometric mean speedup: ~1.45-1.48x across 5 benchmark batch sizes
Score: 71-74 points (out of 100), with Tier 0/1/2 at full marks (35/35)

applied_optimizations.name: Systematic config tuning for all benchmark batch sizes
applied_optimizations.description: Ran exhaustive config search for each benchmark batch size (512-16384). Key changes: M=2048 improved from w=2,s=0 to w=1,s=2 (1.18x->1.27x); M=16384 improved from N=128 to N=256 with K=32 (1.73x->1.78x); M=4096 refined to K=128,w=4,s=0 (stable ~1.80x). M=512 hit ceiling at ~1.18x due to fundamental launch overhead with E=384.

top_bottlenecks: fused_moe_kernel dominates at all batch sizes

hotspot_targets.reason: Dominates GPU time at all batch sizes. M=512 (tpe=11) and M=2048 (tpe=43) are fundamentally limited by low tokens_per_expert causing launch overhead dominance.
hotspot_targets.next_step: Consider alternative kernel dispatch strategies for very low tpe: batched GEMM, custom Triton kernel that processes multiple experts per block, or skip-empty-expert optimization.

next_moves: Consider custom Triton kernel that handles multiple experts per block for small M to reduce launch overhead
Try environment variables like TRITON_CACHE_DIR to ensure consistent Triton compilation
Investigate if padding expert assignments can improve utilization for small M
Profile memory bandwidth utilization to check if memory-bound at small M
notes: Systematic tuning covered 576-1536 configs per batch size
num_warps=1 is optimal for small batch sizes (M<=2048) on AMD MI355X
BLOCK_SIZE_N=256 helps for M=16384 down projection (N=7168)
num_stages=2 with pipelining gives best results for moderate batch sizes
num_stages=0 is best for large compute-bound tiles (M=4096, M=8192)
M=512 hit ceiling at 1.18x - bottleneck is non-kernel overhead (~62us) being ~8% of total time
M=2048 improved from 1.18x to 1.27x by switching to num_warps=1 from num_warps=2
M=16384 improved from 1.73x to 1.78x by using BLOCK_SIZE_N=256 instead of 128
Default config used by test harness is M=64,N=64,K=32,G=8 (not the pathological M=16,N=32,K=64)
Speedup ratio varies ±3% between runs due to GPU scheduling/thermal effects on default time
UP projection: (M*topk, 7168) x (7168, 512), DOWN: (M*topk, 256) x (256, 7168)
override_config forces same config for both UP and DOWN projections
Score consistently around 74-76 across multiple runs
Triton version 3.6.0, GPU: AMD Instinct MI355X

applied_optimizations.name: Separate UP/DOWN projection config support
applied_optimizations.description: Modified try_get_optimal_moe_config to support 'down' key in override_config dict, enabling separate configs for UP (K=7168,N=512) and DOWN (K=256,N=7168) projections. DOWN benefits from N=256, K=32/64 while UP benefits from N=128, K=128. M=4096: 1.75->2.03x, M=8192: 1.49->1.58x, M=16384: 1.73->1.86x.

applied_optimizations.name: Fine-tuned DOWN GROUP_SIZE_M to 4
applied_optimizations.description: Changed DOWN config GROUP_SIZE_M from 8 to 4 for batch sizes 1-8192 based on systematic benchmarking.

attempted_but_failed.name: BLOCK_SIZE_M=128 for M=2048
attempted_but_failed.reason: Stable ~950us but marginally slower than BM=64 on fast path (~930us)

attempted_but_failed.name: num_warps=8 for large batch sizes
attempted_but_failed.reason: Much worse: M=8192 2610us vs 1880us with w=4

hotspot_targets.reason: Dominates GPU time at all batch sizes. UP and DOWN projections have very different shapes (UP: K=7168,N=512 vs DOWN: K=256,N=7168). Separate configs improve DOWN projection significantly.
hotspot_targets.next_step: Consider custom Triton kernel that groups multiple experts per block for M=512 where tpe=11. Also investigate why M=2048 has bimodal performance distribution.

next_moves: Investigate M=2048 bimodal performance (928us or 1010us) - likely caused by expert assignment pattern interactions with DOWN config
Try custom multi-expert Triton kernel for M=512 to reduce launch overhead with 384 experts
Consider fusing activation (silu_and_mul) into DOWN projection kernel to eliminate act_and_mul overhead
Try GPU clock pinning (rocm-smi --setperflevel high) to reduce variance
All config tuning exhausted - configs near-optimal
Consider custom Triton kernel for very small tpe values
Score variance ±3pts is inherent hardware noise (M=4096 default varies 1900-2190us)
notes: Implemented separate UP/DOWN config via 'down' key in override_config dict
DOWN projection benefits from BLOCK_SIZE_N=256 (N=7168) and BLOCK_SIZE_K=32 (K=256)
UP projection benefits from BLOCK_SIZE_N=128 (N=512) and BLOCK_SIZE_K=128 (K=7168)
M=512: ~1.21x ceiling, limited by 384 expert launch overhead with tpe=11
M=2048: bimodal 928us or 1010us with DOWN config, likely expert-assignment-dependent
M=4096: 1.82-1.95x consistently with DOWN config (N256,K32,G8,w4,s2)
M=8192: 1.57-1.59x consistently (UP: N128,K128,G8,w4,s0, DOWN: N256,K64,G8,w4,s0)
GPU: AMD Instinct MI355X, Triton 3.6.0
Score variance is 75-80 due to default config time variance and M=2048 bimodality
torch.profiler shows fused_moe_kernel at 86-96% of cuda_time across batch sizes
rocprof profiling confirms kernel_time > 86% in fused_moe_kernel
ProfilerActivity.CUDA used for GPU kernel timing
DOWN config correctly embedded via 'down' key in UP config, extracted by try_get_optimal_moe_config
Verified: DOWN config gives 5-7% speedup at M=4096/8192/16384 vs using same config for both projections
M=512 ceiling at ~1.21x due to launch overhead with E=384 (tpe=11)
M=2048 bimodal (930us or 1010us) caused by expert assignment patterns, not config
All tuned times rock-solid: M=512 ~782us, M=4096 ~1085us, M=8192 ~1860us, M=16384 ~3089us
Score range: 75-80 depending on M=4096 default variance and M=2048 bimodality
